message:
feat(evaluator): add UTF-8 safe command windowing for long commands

Implement intelligent command windowing that handles multi-byte UTF-8
characters correctly when displaying long commands in terminal output.

Key additions:
- WindowedCommand struct containing display string and adjusted span
- WindowedSpan struct for CHARACTER offsets (vs byte offsets in MatchSpan)
- window_command() function for windowing with configurable max width
- byte_span_to_char_span() for safe byte-to-character offset conversion
- DEFAULT_WINDOW_WIDTH constant (80 characters)

Windowing behavior:
- Centers the matched span within the window when possible
- Adds "..." ellipsis indicators for truncated content
- Preserves UTF-8 character boundaries (never splits multi-byte chars)
- Adjusts span offsets to account for added ellipsis
- Returns None for adjusted_span if original span is invalid

The implementation correctly handles edge cases:
- Commands shorter than max width (no windowing needed)
- Spans at the start, middle, or end of commands
- Multi-byte UTF-8 characters like é, ö, 中文, emoji
- Empty or zero-length spans
- Spans that exceed command length

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
